visa_types.name: Tourist
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/omn/en/lva/travel-insurance)
 The list of documents below is not an exhaustive list of documents and the applicant can always be asked to provide additional information/documents, if desired by the Embassy.
Both the standard and the supporting documentation have to be recent, i.e. not older than 1 month, and need to be provided for every Schengen visa application.
Applicants always have to bring and present their original documents, but they only need to submit photocopies of the same; unless the requirements specify that an original document has
                                                                        to be submitted.
Please click here for the checklist for Tourism
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:  Tourist]
- Applicable visa fee, in OMR (to be paid in cash), is as per the current exchange rate and may be subject to change without notice.
- VFS service fee of 29.800 OMR (Including 5% VAT) is charged per visa application, in addition to the applicable visa fee.
- All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time:
The minimum processing time of a visa application is 15 calendar days, so applicants are advised to leave enough time - between the appointment date and travel date - for the Embassy
                                                                        to process their visa application; no guarantees can be given on the visa application’s return date as delays may occur.
For certain nationalities (see list here (https://www.mfa.gov.lv/en/consular-information/prior-consulation)) the visa application needs consultation from the other Schengen countries and therefore the minimum processing time takes 15 working
                                                                        days.
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete and its documents submitted are as per the Embassy’s requirements (see
                                                                        Documents Required for the different visa categories).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Every applicant, including children/infants, has to submit his/her Schengen visa application in person.
 For Non EU citizens who are not sure whether a Schengen visa is required to travel to the Schengen Area, please click Do I need a visa? (https://www.mfa.gov.lv/en/consular-information/countries-and-territories-whose-citizens-may-enter-latvia-without-a-visa	) to find out.
Applicants, who intend to travel purely for tourist purposes, i.e. visiting for leisure, recreation or sightseeing, and provide for their own accommodation (hotel, hostel, B&B, camping,
                                                                        …) need to apply as Tourist..
 Every Business visa application has to be confirmed by an invitation letter from a business/company based in Latvia, if not, applicant will need to apply as Tourist (#).
                                                                        
 Eligibility 
 Applicant must at least meet the following criteria in order to be eligible to submit a Schengen visa application:
- holding a valid passport/travel document
- holding a valid Oman residence permit.
- applying for a short stay visa: Tourist (including Minors), Visitor Family/Friends, Business, EEA/EU and Swiss national family member or Airport Transit.
Jurisdiction 
Applicants residing in Sultanate of Oman come under the jurisdiction of the Embassy of Latvia in UAE can submit their visa application at the VFS Latvia Visa Application Centre Muscat
                                                                        , which are outsourced representatives for the Embassy of Latvia in Abu Dhabi.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Every applicant, including children/infants, has to submit his/her Schengen visa application in person.
 For Non EU citizens who are not sure whether a Schengen visa is required to travel to the Schengen Area, please click Do I need a visa? (https://www.mfa.gov.lv/en/consular-information/countries-and-territories-whose-citizens-may-enter-latvia-without-a-visa	) to find out.
Applicants, who intend to travel purely for tourist purposes, i.e. visiting for leisure, recreation or sightseeing, and provide for their own accommodation (hotel, hostel, B&B, camping,
                                                                        …) need to apply as Tourist..
 Every Business visa application has to be confirmed by an invitation letter from a business/company based in Latvia, if not, applicant will need to apply as Tourist (#).
                                                                        
 Eligibility 
 Applicant must at least meet the following criteria in order to be eligible to submit a Schengen visa application:
- holding a valid passport/travel document
- holding a valid Oman residence permit.
- applying for a short stay visa: Tourist (including Minors), Visitor Family/Friends, Business, EEA/EU and Swiss national family member or Airport Transit.
Jurisdiction 
Applicants residing in Sultanate of Oman come under the jurisdiction of the Embassy of Latvia in UAE can submit their visa application at the VFS Latvia Visa Application Centre Muscat
                                                                        , which are outsourced representatives for the Embassy of Latvia in Abu Dhabi.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Tourist]
- Applicable visa fee, in OMR (to be paid in cash), is as per the current exchange rate and may be subject to change without notice.
- VFS service fee of 29.800 OMR (Including 5% VAT) is charged per visa application, in addition to the applicable visa fee.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/omn/en/lva/travel-insurance)
 The list of documents below is not an exhaustive list of documents and the applicant can always be asked to provide additional information/documents, if desired by the Embassy.
Both the standard and the supporting documentation have to be recent, i.e. not older than 1 month, and need to be provided for every Schengen visa application.
Applicants always have to bring and present their original documents, but they only need to submit photocopies of the same; unless the requirements specify that an original document has
                                                                        to be submitted.
Please click here for the checklist for Tourism

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Photograph attached (paper clipped) to the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements, then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the
                                                                                photo booth in the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
 The minimum processing time of a visa application is 15 calendar days, so applicants are advised to leave enough time - between the appointment date and travel date - for the Embassy
                                                                        to process their visa application; no guarantees can be given on the visa application’s return date as delays may occur.
For certain nationalities (see list here (https://www.mfa.gov.lv/en/consular-information/prior-consulation)) the visa application needs consultation from the other Schengen countries and therefore the minimum processing time takes 15 working
                                                                        days.
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete and its documents submitted are as per the Embassy’s requirements (see
                                                                        Documents Required for the different visa categories).

## Online Application Form
 For Latvia, every applicant needs to provide a signed printout of the Schengen visa application form that has to be completed online (https://epak.pmlp.gov.lv/NVIS.EService001.WebSite/).

## Photo Specifications

## Processing Time

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you want to work or study in Latvia for longer than 90 days, you should apply for a Long-term D Category Visa.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you want to work or study in Latvia for longer than 90 days, you should apply for a Long-term D Category Visa.

## Visa Fees

[Table:Long Term D Category Visa]
- Applicable visa fee, in OMR (to be paid in cash and credit card), is as per the current exchange rate and may be subject to change without notice.
- VFS service fee of 29.800 OMR (Including 5% VAT) is charged per visa application, in addition to the applicable visa fee.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
The list of documents below is not an exhaustive list of documents and the applicant can always be asked to provide additional information/documents, if desired by the Embassy.
Both the standard and the supporting documentation have to be recent, i.e. not older than 1 month, and need to be provided for every Long term visa application.
Applicants always have to bring and present their original documents, but they only need to submit photocopies of the same; unless the requirements specify that an original document has to be submitted.
Please find below the checklists for Long Term D visa category-
D Visa Students category 1_Checklist Latvia

D Visa Students category 2_Checklist Latvia

D Visa Work category 1_Checklist Latvia

D Visa Work category 2_Checklist Latvia

QUESTIONNAIRE

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Photograph attached (paper clipped) to the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements, then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
The minimum processing time of a visa application is 15 calendar days, so applicants are advised to leave enough time - between the appointment date and travel date - for the Embassy to process their visa application; no guarantees can be given on the visa application’s return date as delays may occur.
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete and its documents submitted are as per the Embassy’s requirements (see Documents Required for the different visa categories).

## Download Forms
Every applicant needs to provide a printout of the fully completed and signed Long Stay Visa Application Form of the Republic of Latvia:
https://epak.pmlp.gov.lv/NVIS.EService001.WebSite/ (https://epak.pmlp.gov.lv/NVIS.EService001.WebSite/)

- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/omn/en/lva/travel-insurance)
 The list of documents below is not an exhaustive list of documents and the applicant can always be asked to provide additional information/documents, if desired by the Embassy.
Both the standard and the supporting documentation have to be recent, i.e. not older than 1 month, and need to be provided for every Schengen visa application.
Applicants always have to bring and present their original documents, but they only need to submit photocopies of the same; unless the requirements specify that an original document has
                                                                        to be submitted.
Please click here for the checklist for Visiting Family and Friends

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Photograph attached (paper clipped) to the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements, then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the
                                                                                photo booth in the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
 The minimum processing time of a visa application is 15 calendar days, so applicants are advised to leave enough time - between the appointment date and travel date - for the Embassy
                                                                        to process their visa application; no guarantees can be given on the visa application’s return date as delays may occur.
For certain nationalities (see list here (https://www.mfa.gov.lv/en/consular-information/prior-consulation)) the visa application needs consultation from the other Schengen countries and therefore the minimum processing time takes 15 working
                                                                        days.
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete and its documents submitted are as per the Embassy’s requirements (see
                                                                        Documents Required for the different visa categories).

## Online Application Form
 For Latvia, every applicant needs to provide a signed printout of the Schengen visa application form that has to be completed online (https://epak.pmlp.gov.lv/NVIS.EService001.WebSite/).

Documents to be provided by all visa applicants
 The list of documents below is not an exhaustive list of documents and the applicant can always be asked to provide additional information/documents, if desired by the Embassy.
Both the standard and the supporting documentation have to be recent, i.e. not older than 1 month, and need to be provided for every Schengen visa application.

[Table:  Business]
- Applicable visa fee, in OMR (to be paid in cash), is as per the current exchange rate and may be subject to change without notice.
- VFS service fee of 29.800 OMR (Including 5% VAT) is charged per visa application, in addition to the applicable visa fee.
- All fees are non-refundable.

                                                                        to process their visa application; no guarantees can be given on the visa application’s return date as delays may occur.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Every applicant, including children/infants, has to submit his/her Schengen visa application in person.
 For Non EU citizens who are not sure whether a Schengen visa is required to travel to the Schengen Area, please click Do I need a visa? (https://www.mfa.gov.lv/en/consular-information/countries-and-territories-whose-citizens-may-enter-latvia-without-a-visa	) to find out.
 Applicants, who intend to travel for business purposes, i.e. visiting a business/company to have meetings, to make business deals, to recruit, to train or other work related activities
                                                                        can apply as Business.
 Every Business visa application has to be confirmed by an invitation letter from a business/company based in Latvia, if not, applicant will need to apply as Tourist (#).
                                                                        
 Eligibility 
 Applicant must at least meet the following criteria in order to be eligible to submit a Schengen visa application:
- holding a valid passport/travel document
- holding a valid Oman residence permit.
- applying for a short stay visa: Tourist (including Minors), Visitor Family/Friends, Business, EEA/EU and Swiss national family member or Airport Transit.
Jurisdiction 
 Applicants residing in Sultanate of Oman come under the jurisdiction of the Embassy of Latvia in UAE can submit their visa application at the VFS Latvia Visa Application Muscat, which
                                                                        are outsourced representatives for the Embassy of Latvia in Abu Dhabi.
visa_types.full_text:

Every applicant, including children/infants, has to submit his/her Schengen visa application in person.
 For Non EU citizens who are not sure whether a Schengen visa is required to travel to the Schengen Area, please click Do I need a visa? (https://www.mfa.gov.lv/en/consular-information/countries-and-territories-whose-citizens-may-enter-latvia-without-a-visa	) to find out.

                                                                        can apply as Business.
 Every Business visa application has to be confirmed by an invitation letter from a business/company based in Latvia, if not, applicant will need to apply as Tourist (#).

- applying for a short stay visa: Tourist (including Minors), Visitor Family/Friends, Business, EEA/EU and Swiss national family member or Airport Transit.
